macro_views.name: US Growth
macro_views.lean: Running around neutral, Oil shock sterilizes growth impulse from tax cuts + rebates.
signals: ISM New Orders rocketed higher into expansion territory & Initial jobless claims remain low. Fiscal Deficit 7%.
Corporate earnings remain strong and looking to be double digits. + corporate fiscal balances.
FCI is supportive. Wealth effect and easy credit conditions (low credit spreads)
macro_views.counter: Tail risk if hiring picks up. Consumer already hit by rising headline inflation from Energy prices.

macro_views.name: Global Growth
macro_views.lean: Middle East war looks to be wrapping up. Energy prices higher will hit global growth, but we have global fiscal stimulus which can make up for the difference.
signals: Oil prices have fallen below 100, so this too shall pass
Dollar is starting to sell off, and risk is being bid up. Market is telling us things will be better.
Stimulus still there from US, EMU, and China.
macro_views.counter: Economic growth has taken several hits and IMF has revised lower their estimates of global growth.

macro_views.name: Inflation
macro_views.lean: Headline came in hot. This ties the Fed's hands for the next couple meetings. Core still very tame which gives them an out to keep cutting.
macro_views.counter: Shelter Inflation coming in weaker. Tariffs are far less impactful than headline figures and energy shock could quickly reverse.

macro_views.name: Fed Policy Path
macro_views.lean: Still think the Fed will cut. >100 NFP report was a fluke and it will take time for the labor market to heal. Still digesting Capex wave from AI.
signals: Wage growth remains low. Breadth of job gains is quite poor.
unemployed to job openings has decreased quite a bit, up in last report around 1
macro_views.counter: ISM in expansion, and if a cyclical upswing is real, hiring should follow.

macro_views.name: Term Premium
macro_views.lean: More steepening. The Fed wants to cut, and we are spending irresponsibly. In the near-term, bonds provide a hedge which will be bid.

macro_views.name: Credit
macro_views.lean: Leak higher but solid positive excess returns over a year. Defaults increase but limited contagion from problem sectors. Private Credit will disappoint for the next couple quarters, as market learns how evergreen work.
signals: Loans have generated Positive TR YTD despite poor sentiment.
Fundamentals can improve now that war is over
macro_views.counter: SAAS apocalypse dents loan market and direct lending. Large redemptions continue out of evergreen direct lending vehicles.

macro_views.name: USD
macro_views.lean: stable. Clearly still a safe-haven. Will leak lower with solid global growth.
signals: yields higher in Germany but dollar still up, on some trading days
US Assets outperforming RoW through this shock, energy indepdence.
macro_views.counter: Institutions continue to be degraded by uni-lateral military action.  medium term dollar bearish.

asset_views.name: Stocks vs Bonds
asset_views.note: Nominal growth is solid and plenty of room for earnings to be
asset_views.commentary: So I can reset data, but not upload old data. I want to make it easier to pass along data and make updates. Make sure this is considered and develop a framework to do this.

asset_views.name: US LC
asset_views.note: Rerating of Mag-7 good entry point & index has gone sideways form 6 months.

asset_views.name: US SMID
asset_views.note: Bad cycle position. Could benefit from cyclical upswing, if it comes.

asset_views.name: Europe
asset_views.note: Watch out for gas prices. Very vulnerable to Energy shock.

asset_views.name: Japan
asset_views.note: Playing with fire politically. Doesn't look insulated from the geopolitical vol.

asset_views.name: EM x China
asset_views.note: Medium term, main beneficiary from a weak dollar. Need better growth picture to get overweight. Korea companies will grow earnings as capex cycle continues.

asset_views.name: China
asset_views.note: Innovation sector. China slow bull market.

asset_views.name: Private Equity
asset_views.note: Software is challenged but good alternative to public markets. Growth/VC deliver AI play.

asset_views.name: US Treasuries
asset_views.note: High real yields. Growth outlooks looks better so

asset_views.name: Global Bonds
asset_views.note: Prefer USD through this volatility. Medium term bullish.

asset_views.name: EM Debt
asset_views.note: Spreads are way too tight for EM$. EM local longer term play.

projects.name: Regime Models
projects.status: Getting up and running with Skills and Agents has been challenging. PowerShell is annoying.
projects.next_step: Systematic Investment Strategy with various portfolios. Hopefully something implementable with futures.

projects.name: Term Premium
projects.status: Backtest looks great. Running Paper trading account. Decide whether to trade on it.

reconciliations.macro_scan: Been a tough week. Got this Journal up and running.
reconciliations.quant_check: Built out Term Structure Model, Feature Analysis.
reconciliations.synthesis: Learned that CARRY is a huge determinant in trading the curve. Drives 2/3rds of the alpha in my strategy.

briefing.top_of_mind: Trump has TACO'ed, that is priced into markets. Mid-terms mean he has to pivot to pro-growth policy agenda. Arguably we get back to fundamentals and valuation. Question is whether leadership continues, where broadening out of earnings continues, and IT multiples get challenged. Private markets & SAAS is in the cross hairs.
